diffs.patch:
Index: bench-artifacts/code-review-diagnosis.md
===================================================================
--- bench-artifacts/code-review-diagnosis.md	
+++ bench-artifacts/code-review-diagnosis.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,157 @@
+# Code Review Diagnosis
+
+## File: bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs
+
+## Findings
+
+### 1. Missing Amount Validation
+
+**Location:** `chargeCustomer` and `refundCustomer` functions (lines 1-9)
+
+**Issue:** No validation of the `amount` parameter before gateway operations.
+
+**Risks:**
+- Null/undefined amounts can cause API failures
+- Negative amounts may be accepted (security/logic flaw)
+- Decimal precision issues can accumulate incorrectly
+- Currency formatting not enforced
+- Maximum transaction limits not checked
+
+**Impact:** Invalid transactions, corrupted charge records, potential security vulnerabilities
+
+### 2. Missing Gateway Error Handling
+
+**Location:** All gateway invocations (lines 3, 8)
+
+**Issue:** Gateway calls assume synchronous, always-successful operations without error checks.
+
+**Risks:**
+- Line 3: `gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)` - No return value or error check
+- Line 8: `gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)` - No return value or error check
+- Card declined, expired card, insufficient funds, network timeout all silently succeed
+- No error propagation to caller
+- No retry mechanism for transient failures
+- No transaction ID returned on success
+- No rollback capability on failure
+
+**Impact:** Silent failures break financial consistency, customers unaware of failed transactions, audit trail corrupted
+
+### 3. Missing Customer Validation
+
+**Location:** `chargeCustomer` function only (lines 2-4)
+
+**Issue:** Only checks `customer.id` exists but validates nothing else.
+
+**Risks:**
+- `refundCustomer` has no customer validation at all (line 7)
+- Invalid customer reference can cause unpredictable behavior
+- No existence check (customer may be deleted)
+- No active status check (suspended customers?)
+- No API token validation if needed
+
+**Impact:** Refunds to non-existent customers, charges on invalid accounts
+
+### 4. Missing Return Type Contract
+
+**Location:** Both functions (lines 4, 9)
+
+**Issue:** `chargeCustomer` returns `{ ok: true }` unconditionally, `refundCustomer` always `{ ok: true }`.
+
+**Risks:**
+- Caller cannot distinguish success from failure
+- Error information lost completely
+- No `ok: false` path exists even when charges/refunds fail
+- Return object structure is misleading
+
+**Impact:** Application logic breaks, transactions appear successful when they failed
+
+## Residual Risks
+
+### Critical (Must Fix)
+
+1. **Gateway errors unhandled** - Financial transactions failing silently is unacceptable
+2. **Amount not validated** - Any value passed will be attempted
+
+### High Priority
+
+3. **Customer validation incomplete** - Refund function has no validation at all
+4. **Return values don't reflect actual outcomes** - No error codes
+
+### Medium Priority
+
+5. **No transaction IDs** - Cannot track/refund specific charges
+6. **No retry logic** - Transient network failures cause failures
+
+### Recommendations
+
+```javascript
+export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
+  // Add amount validation
+  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer', code: 'MISSING_CUSTOMER' }
+  if (amount === null || amount === undefined) {
+    return { ok: false, error: 'invalid amount', code: 'INVALID_AMOUNT', message: 'Amount is required' }
+  }
+  if (typeof amount !== 'number' || amount <= 0) {
+    return { ok: false, error: 'invalid amount', code: 'INVALID_AMOUNT', message: 'Amount must be positive' }
+  }
+  // Add amount limits
+  // const MAX_AMOUNT = 999999
+  // if (amount > MAX_AMOUNT) return { ok: false, error: 'amount exceeded', code: 'AMOUNT_EXCEEDED' }
+  
+  // Actually execute charge with error handling
+  const result = gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
+  if (!result.success && !result.recovered) {
+    return { ok: false, error: 'charge_failed', code: result.error.code, message: result.error.message }
+  }
+  return { 
+    ok: true, 
+    transactionId: result.id,
+    amountCharged: result.actualAmount
+  }
+}
+
+export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
+  // Add customer validation
+  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer', code: 'MISSING_CUSTOMER', message: 'Customer ID is required' }
+  if (!customer.enabled) return { ok: false, error: 'customer disabled', code: 'CUSTOMER_DISABLED', message: 'Customer account is suspended' }
+  
+  // Add amount validation
+  if (amount === null || amount === undefined) {
+    return { ok: false, error: 'invalid amount', code: 'INVALID_AMOUNT', message: 'Refund amount is required' }
+  }
+  if (amount <= 0) {
+    return { ok: false, error: 'invalid amount', code: 'INVALID_AMOUNT', message: 'Refund amount must be positive' }
+  }
+  
+  // Attempt refund with error handling
+  const result = gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
+  if (!result.success && !result.recovered) {
+    return { ok: false, error: 'refund_failed', code: result.error.code, message: result.error.message }
+  }
+  return { 
+    ok: true, 
+    transactionId: result.id,
+    amountRefunded: result.actualAmount
+  }
+}
+```
+
+### Verification Commands (to run after fixes)
+
+```bash
+# Test validation boundaries
+node -e '
+const payment = require("./payment.mjs");
+console.log("Test null amount:", payment.chargeCustomer({id: "test"}, null));
+console.log("Test negative amount:", payment.chargeCustomer({id: "test"}, -100));
+console.log("Refund validation:", payment.refundCustomer({id: null}, 50));
+'
+
+# Mock gateway failures
+./scripts/test-payment-gateway-errors.sh
+```
+
+---
+**Reviewed:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`  
+**Severity Summary:** 4 critical/high issues identified  
+**Estimated Risk:** **High** - Production use unsafe
\ No newline at end of file
